name: libdevel-callchecker-perl
title: libdevel-callchecker-perl
summary: custom op checking attached to subroutines
description:
custom op checking attached to subroutines
Devel::CallChecker makes some new features of the Perl 5.14.0 C API
available to XS modules running on older versions of Perl.
The features are centred around the function cv_set_call_checker,
which allows XS code to attach a magical annotation
to a Perl subroutine,
resulting in resolvable calls to that subroutine
being mutated at compile time by arbitrary C code.
This module makes cv_set_call_checker
and several supporting functions available.
(It is possible to achieve the effect of cv_set_call_checker
from XS code on much earlier Perl versions,
but it is painful to achieve without the centralised facility.)
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libdevel-callchecker-perl because custom op checking attached to subroutines.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libdevel-callchecker-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libdevel-callchecker-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libdevel-callchecker-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
